title: Interpret Overall System Bag Out Control Options
summary: Use the source slide content to interpret the documented meaning of the Overall System Bag Out Control options, including system bag out, bag out by sorter leg, cancel bag out for manual bag out, and pre bag out.
when_to_use: Use this reference when reviewing the Overall System Bag Out Control slide or equivalent documented source content and you need to understand what each listed bag out option means according to the training material.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as an execution procedure for performing bag out actions.
Do not use this runbook to infer button paths, popup behavior, navigation steps, or system responses beyond the source-provided descriptions.
Do not use this runbook if the available screen, popup, or control labels do not match the source terminology.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the source slide or frame and interpret each bag out option using only the documented descriptions.

safety_notes: Use only the source-provided descriptions when interpreting bag out options.
Do not infer execution steps, button paths, or system responses beyond the source-provided descriptions.
Escalate if the available screen, popup, or control labels do not match the source terminology.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the training slide or equivalent documented bag out control reference
Ability to view the bag out control terminology used in the source

access_required: Access to the training video slide or frame for Overall System Bag Out Control
system_preconditions: The user can view the source terminology for the bag out control options being discussed

visual_references.description: Training slide frame titled Overall System Bag Out Control showing the documented descriptions for system bag out, bag out by sorter leg, cancel bag out, and pre bag out.

steps.title: Locate Overall System Bag Out Control information
steps.purpose: Identify the source material that defines the bag out control options.
steps.instruction: Locate the "Overall System Bag Out Control" information in the source and review the slide or frame that contains the option descriptions.
steps.expected_result: The correct source slide or frame is visible for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The slide title and bullet content match the Overall System Bag Out Control reference in the source.
steps.failure_condition: The slide, popup, or control labels do not match the source terminology or the referenced content cannot be located.

steps.title: Interpret system bag out behavior
steps.purpose: Capture the documented meaning of the system bag out option.
steps.instruction: Identify the documented "system bag out" behavior and note that it will close all destinations and exchange all totes with parcels in them.
steps.expected_result: The meaning of system bag out is recorded exactly as described in the source.
steps.healthy_condition: System bag out is interpreted as closing all destinations and exchanging all totes with parcels in them.
steps.failure_condition: The interpretation adds unsupported execution details or differs from the source wording.

actions.label: Read system bag out description
actions.exact_action: Identify the documented 'system bag out' behavior and note that it will close all destinations and exchange all totes with parcels in them.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Bullet text describing that system bag out closes all destinations and exchanges all totes with parcels in them.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed description does not match the source wording for system bag out.
Additional behavior must be assumed because the source description is insufficient for the intended use.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: System bag out will close all destinations and exchange all totes with parcels in them; a user could also choose to bag out by leg of the sorter.

steps.title: Interpret bag out by sorter leg
steps.purpose: Capture the documented meaning of the sorter-leg bag out scope option.
steps.instruction: Identify the documented option to bag out by leg of the sorter and note that the source presents this as a user-selectable scope.
steps.expected_result: The meaning of bag out by sorter leg is recorded as a selectable scope described by the source.
steps.healthy_condition: The interpretation stays limited to the source statement that a user can choose to bag out by leg of the sorter.
steps.failure_condition: The interpretation assumes unsupported details about how the leg is selected or what additional system behavior occurs.

actions.label: Read sorter leg option
actions.exact_action: Identify the documented option to bag out by leg of the sorter and note that the source presents this as a user-selectable scope.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Bullet text stating that a user could choose to bag out by leg of the sorter.

stop_or_escalate_if: The available control labels do not match the source terminology for bag out by leg of the sorter.
Selection behavior or scope must be inferred beyond the source statement.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: System bag out will close all destinations and exchange all totes with parcels in them; a user could also choose to bag out by leg of the sorter.

steps.title: Interpret cancel bag out for manual bag out
steps.purpose: Capture the documented meaning of the cancel bag out option in a manual bag out context.
steps.instruction: Identify the documented "cancel bag out" behavior for manual bag out and note that it will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.
steps.expected_result: The meaning of cancel bag out is recorded exactly as described in the source.
steps.healthy_condition: Cancel bag out is interpreted as canceling any exchange task that is not in process.
steps.failure_condition: The interpretation adds unsupported details about tasks already in process, user actions, or system workflow.

actions.label: Read cancel bag out description
actions.exact_action: Identify the documented 'cancel bag out' behavior for manual bag out and note that it will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Bullet text describing cancel bag out for manual bag out and its effect on exchange tasks not in process.

stop_or_escalate_if: The available terminology for manual bag out or cancel bag out does not match the source.
The user needs behavior details beyond the source statement about exchange tasks not in process.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In the event of a manual bag out Fortna offers a cancel bag out that will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.

steps.title: Interpret pre bag out behavior
steps.purpose: Capture the documented meaning of the pre bag out option.
steps.instruction: Identify the documented "pre bag out" behavior and note that it exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.
steps.expected_result: The meaning of pre bag out is recorded exactly as described in the source.
steps.healthy_condition: Pre bag out is interpreted as exchanging the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.
steps.failure_condition: The interpretation changes the percentage, scope, or exchange behavior from the source wording.

actions.label: Read pre bag out description
actions.exact_action: Identify the documented 'pre bag out' behavior and note that it exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.

Additional operational detail is required beyond the source statement.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide title: Overall System Bag Out Control. System bag out will close all destinations and exchange all totes with parcels in them; a user could also choose to bag out by leg of the sorter.
In the event of a manual bag out Fortna offers a cancel bag out that will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.
Pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.

success_criteria: The user can distinguish the documented effect of system bag out, bag out by sorter leg, cancel bag out, and pre bag out.
All recorded meanings remain limited to the source-provided descriptions.
healthy_conditions: The Overall System Bag Out Control source is available and matches the referenced terminology.
Each bag out option is interpreted using the exact source-supported meaning.
failure_conditions: The available screen, popup, or control labels do not match the source terminology.
The user attempts to infer execution steps, button paths, or system responses beyond the source-provided descriptions.
The source content needed to interpret an option cannot be confirmed.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the available screen, popup, or control labels do not match the source terminology.
Escalate if an operational decision requires execution steps, button paths, or system responses not provided by this source.
Escalate for SME review if the source wording is insufficient for the intended operational use.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Slide title: Overall System Bag Out Control. System bag out will close all destinations and exchange all totes with parcels in them; a user could also choose to bag out by leg of the sorter.
In the event of a manual bag out Fortna offers a cancel bag out that will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.
Pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.

missing_details: The source provides descriptive behavior only and does not provide explicit execution steps.
The source does not provide button names, navigation paths, or popup interaction steps for these bag out options.
The source does not provide role boundaries beyond general operator-oriented training context.
The source does not provide time estimates, production stop requirements, or LOTO requirements.
